title: oxydectes erythroxyloides
intro: Oxydectes erythroxyloides, also known as the Golden Ball Tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Achariaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid and glycoside content. The health benefits of Oxydectes erythroxyloides include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a diuretic and expectorant. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.

The essential oils extracted from Oxydectes erythroxyloides have been shown to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating wounds, infections, and skin conditions like acne and eczema.
